rows.task: Our checkout API returns HTTP 500 for logged-in users but works fine for anonymous users; the database is healthy. Find the root cause.
Design an onboarding flow for a developer CLI tool that genuinely delights users — something not done before.
rows.task_type: open-ended design / novelty ideation (create a delightful, never-before-done CLI onboarding flow)
plan: first_principles: Strip onboarding to its actual goal (first-run delight + fast activation) and challenge the default CLI paradigm (install -> config -> hello-world tutorial). 'Not done before' demands rejecting the inherited template before generating anything.

analogical: Import first-encounter delight mechanics from other fields that have solved this — game first-levels/tutorials-as-play, hospitality/concierge welcomes, unboxing rituals, magic tricks/reveals — to seed genuinely fresh CLI patterns.
divergent: Generate multiple concrete, novel onboarding-flow concepts from the reframed goal + borrowed mechanics; push for surprising, delightful options rather than one safe wizard.
convergent_verify: Select and pin down the strongest flow; check it actually delights, activates quickly, respects CLI/terminal constraints, and is buildable — finalize the design.

rows.task: Estimate how many piano tuners are working in Chicago.
rows.task_type: Fermi estimation under uncertainty — a quantitative estimate with no supplied data, requiring a structured multiplicative breakdown with order-of-magnitude reasoning on each factor.
plan: decompose: Break the estimate into a multiplicative chain of sub-quantities — Chicago population, people per household, fraction of households owning a piano (plus institutions: schools, churches, venues), tunings per piano per year, and a single tuner's annual job capacity (tunings/day x working days). This converts one unanswerable question into a few separately-estimable factors.

bayesian: Assign a point estimate plus a rough plausible range to each factor under uncertainty (e.g., ~2.7M people, ~2.5/household, ~1 in 20 owning a piano, ~1 tuning/year, ~4 tunings/day x ~200 days). Propagate them: total annual tunings demanded divided by tunings one tuner performs per year yields the tuner count, carrying the uncertainty so the answer is an order-of-magnitude band, not a false-precision number.

convergent_verify: Sanity-check the result against the famous benchmark (~tens, roughly 30-100 tuners), confirm units cancel correctly through the chain, test sensitivity to the shakiest assumptions (piano-ownership rate and tunings/year), and state the final figure as a justified range with its key drivers.

rows.task: Prove that the sum of the first n odd numbers equals n squared.
After we changed our checkout button from grey to green, sales rose 12%. Did the color change cause the increase?
rows.task_type: causal attribution (correlation-vs-causation inference under incomplete data)
plan: causal: frame the question as causation vs. mere correlation — the 12% rise is an observed before/after association, not an established effect. Articulate the counterfactual (what would sales have done WITHOUT the recolor?), the absence of a control/A-B split, and the confounding structure (anything that co-moved with the change). This mode anchors the whole task.

abductive: enumerate competing root-cause explanations for the sales rise and pressure-test each against the color hypothesis — seasonality/holiday timing, concurrent marketing or pricing changes, traffic-mix shifts, novelty effect, regression to the mean, the recolor being bundled with other UI/copy edits, or simply random variance. Identify what evidence would discriminate among them.

convergent_verify: converge on a calibrated verdict — likely 'not established from this data alone' — and specify what WOULD establish causation (a randomized A/B test on button color, holding all else constant; or difference-in-differences against a control segment), plus the minimal data needed (sample size, significance, baseline trend).

rows.task: Should we rewrite our monolith as microservices, or keep the monolith? Decide.
rows.task_type: Contested binary trade-off decision (architecture: monolith vs. microservices)

rows.task: From these examples infer the rule and compute f(10): f(2)=5, f(3)=7, f(4)=9.
A server intermittently drops 1% of requests only during the nightly backup window. Diagnose it.
rows.task_type: abductive_diagnosis (root-cause debugging of an intermittent, time-correlated failure)

rows.task: We keep proposing the same three caching strategies. Give us a genuinely novel approach nobody on the team has tried.
rows.task_type: creative_ideation_unstick (generate a genuinely novel design while escaping a solution rut)

rows.task: Plan the migration of a 200-table Postgres database to a new schema with zero downtime.
rows.task_type: Engineering execution-plan for a constrained, high-risk database migration (zero-downtime schema change across 200 tables)
